purpose: Transparent stone-resource presentation icon with faithful runtime derivatives for the separately reviewed Alpha 0.3.7 shared-world resource authority; the artwork alone is not evidence of a balance, currency, reward, or entitlement.

projectAuthorization.scope: Add the background-cleaned stone icon to Warpkeep PR #45 and permit its future official Warpkeep Pages runtime use if the separately reviewed mechanics are implemented.
notGranted: general third-party derivative or redistribution permission
currency, reward, value, or entitlement rights

sourceInputs.runtimeUse: visual composition and stone-resource icon style reference only

generation.prompt:
Use case: background-extraction
Asset type: transparent in-game stone resource icon for Warpkeep
Input image: edit target — the supplied illustrated stack of gray quarry blocks, slabs, and boulders.
Primary request: Preserve the stone subject, composition, proportions, gray rock palette, highlights, cracks, silhouettes, and painterly game-art style exactly. Replace only the checkerboard background with a perfectly flat solid #00ffff cyan chroma-key background for subsequent removal.
Composition/framing: keep the complete stone stack centered and fully visible with generous even padding. Do not crop or add objects.
Lighting/mood: preserve the existing neutral-to-warm rim lighting on the stones only.
Constraints: change only the background. Keep the stone subject cleanly separated from it with crisp anti-aliased edges. The background must be exactly uniform #00ffff, with no shadows, gradients, texture, reflections, floor plane, lighting variation, watermark, text, glow, or cast/contact shadow. Do not use #00ffff anywhere in the stone artwork.

referenceAsset.delivery: checked-in provenance master outside public/; only the exact reviewed 64 px faithful derivatives below are served by Pages

runtimeDerivative.authorizationBasis: The separately reviewed Alpha 0.3.7 resource authority activates the previously recorded owner authorization for official Warpkeep Pages runtime use.

installation.family: all eight Hegemony resource runtime outputs

licence.policy: The exact project instruction authorizes the recorded Warpkeep use but does not establish a public relicensing grant. See ASSETS-LICENSE.md.
